title: Executive, Pathway
description:
Job Description

We are seeking for a dynamic and experienced individual to join our team and play a pivotal role in the development of badminton in Singapore. This position is central to the planning, implementation, and advancement of pathway initiatives across regional training centres, coaching pathway development programmes, and participation programmes for youth and seniors.

Reporting Structure

To the Pathway Manager or person assigned by the Head of Pathways & Performance to carry out all roles & responsibilities in consultation with the reporting personnel.

Key Responsibilities:

1. Pathway Development & Management

  • Assist in pathway initiatives linked to the Regional Training Centre (RTC), including talent identification and athlete development benchmarks.
  • Coordinate with Regional Training Centre (RTC) on court bookings and ensure compliance with established rules and regulations.

2. Coaching Pathway & Capability Development

  • Develop and manage structured coaching pathway programmes in collaboration with CoachSG.
  • Support the identification, development, and progression of coaches across grassroots, development, and high-performance levels.
  • Work closely with senior coaches to ensure coach education programmes reflect best practices and evolving performance needs.
  • Assist in a national coaching membership framework, including the maintenance of a comprehensive and up-to-date database of registered coaches.
  • Establish coach profiling, accreditation, and competency benchmarks to support appropriate deployment across youth and senior programmes.
  • Coordinate the identification and allocation of coaches from the coaching membership pool to support grassroots, developmental, and community-based programmes.

Other Duties:

  • When SBA organized any tournaments or selection trials, to assist in the general secretariat functions for the tournament.
  • As and when assigned by CEO / Head of Pathways & Performance

Requirements:

  • Diploma, A Level or Degree with at least one year of relevant working experience in sports
  • Highly organized with the ability to manage multiple priorities simultaneously.
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to engage effectively with stakeholders.
  • Able to work in a fast-paced, dynamic environment with minimal supervision.
  • Flexibility to work irregular hours and travel as needed during events, including weekends and holidays.

postedCompany.description:
Singapore Badminton Association (SBA) has been the national governing body for badminton in Singapore since 1929. For over 90 years, we have been dedicated to growing the sport, building a strong community, and putting Singapore on the world badminton map.

From organising tournaments to developing new talent, our mission has always been to nurture the sport at every level. We are proud members of the Badminton World Federation (BWF) and the Badminton Asia Confederation (BAC), and are affiliated with the Singapore National Olympic Council (SNOC).

Today, with 32 member clubs and an elected Management Committee led by our President, Mr Lawrence Leow, we continue to honour our legacy while shaping the future of badminton in Singapore.
postedCompany.name: Singapore Badminton Association
